name: sliver
summary: Sliver is an open source cross-platform adversary emulation/red team framework, it can be used by organizations of all sizes to perform security testing. Sliver's implants support C2 over Mutual TLS (mTLS), WireGuard, HTTP(S), and DNS and are dynamically compiled with per-binary asymmetric encryption keys.

documentation.readme: Sliver is an open source cross-platform adversary emulation/red team framework, it can be used by organizations of all sizes to perform security testing. Sliver's implants support C2 over Mutual TLS (mTLS), WireGuard, HTTP(S), and DNS and are dynamically compiled with per-binary asymmetric encryption keys.
documentation.how_to_use: Run the primary entrypoint as shown in upstream docs, e.g. `cd toolboxes/sliver && python3 sliver.py --help`. Further invocations from upstream: `cd toolboxes/sliver && cat README.md`; `cd toolboxes/sliver && ls -la`. trust.warning: Attribution/index entry only. Audit provenance, code, releases and licence before use.

use_cases: Index entry in the Command and Control category. Use as a candidate tool in the chain planner after a NVD/lookup pass on the target vendor.
Pairs with the Command and Control system-prompt stanza in core.ai_backend.chain to teach the LLM the standard invocation pattern.
Operator-initiated: the chain step is gated by the per-step ACCEPT/CANCEL gate; the tool does not auto-execute.
Use as a reference implementation when designing a new v3 method for this category.
Use as a teaching example in the prompt stanza so the LLM can reason about the right run order.
